title: Fragrant Earthy Portuguese Lamb Stew
description: A hearty and aromatic stew featuring tender lamb, earthy spices, and a touch of English mustard, all slow-cooked to perfection in a hot pot vessel, with a unique spherification twist, making it a show-off Portuguese dish that is also ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the hot pot vessel over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the lamb cubes and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the English mustard, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the water to the hot pot vessel and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Stir in the sodium alginate and continue to simmer for an additional 10 minutes, or until the stew has thickened slightly.
Use a syringe or spoon to drop small amounts of the stew into a bath of cold water to create spheres, if desired.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh herbs or crusty bread, if desired.
